title: Epistaxis review in GP

red_flags.text: Supports documenting onset, active or recent bleeding, severity, site, anticoagulant or antiplatelet context, bleeding disorder, vital observations, and clinician concern without generating haemostasis or medicine instructions.
red_flags.rationale: The exact epistaxis section supports interval bleeding onset, laterality, frequency, duration, estimated severity, recurrence, trauma, medicines, bleeding risk, and impact without assigning the source or stability.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting onset, active or recent bleeding, severity, site, anticoagulant or antiplatelet context, bleeding disorder, vital observations, and clinician concern without generating haemostasis or medicine instructions.

exact_section.heading: Key action statements 1–5 — prompt assessment, bleeding site, and risk factors
exact_section.locator: official fact sheet key action statements 1–5

red_flags.text: Supports documenting recurrence, laterality, clinician-performed nasal examination, already completed endoscopy context, risk factors, prevention discussion, patient questions, outcome review, and follow-up without ordering procedures.
red_flags.rationale: The exact recurrent-exam section supports clinician-performed nasal assessment, recurrent or unilateral context, documented concern, existing records, clinician discussion, and follow-up without implying endoscopy or generating treatment.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting recurrence, laterality, clinician-performed nasal examination, already completed endoscopy context, risk factors, prevention discussion, patient questions, outcome review, and follow-up without ordering procedures.

exact_section.heading: Key action statements 6–14 — recurrent bleed assessment, examination, risk factors, education, and outcomes
exact_section.locator: official fact sheet key action statements 6–14

sources_used.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Nosebleed (Epistaxis)
sources_used.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE emergency, ENT, haematology, procedure, and prescribing adaptation

source_limitations: Current bleeding, volume, haemodynamics, trauma, anticoagulation, nasal findings, anaemia, source, diagnosis, procedure, medicine, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
